PAGE.p: Released versions of OpenConnect are available from the FTP site, and also over HTTP:
Release tarballs (since v3.13) are signed with the PGP key with fingerprint BE07 D9FD 5480 9AB2 C4B0 FF5F 6376 2CDA 67E2 F359.

From v9.23 onwards, releases will also include a "-nogen" version of the signed tarball. This contains only pristine files from the git repository, without any autogenerated autoconf files. Distribution packagers may prefer to use this version and run ./autogen.sh manually as part of the packaging process... although you should carefully consider the question of whether you're just trusting everything signed with the release PGP key anyway; some might argue that the -nogen releases are only useful if you actually compare them to what's in the git tree, and in that case you might as well just have been building from the git tag.
Nevertheless, the changes needed to make the -nogen tarballs build cleanly are also useful to allow you to build directly from git with the version reported cleanly and correctly.
The latest release is OpenConnect v9.21 (PGP signature), released on 2026-06-16 with the following changelog:

ul.li: Fix infinite loop in buf_append() when output exactly fills available buffer space (#851).
Accept base32 token secrets without trailing = padding (#771).
Fix crash on NULL passphrase from TPM2 auth dialog (#842).

PAGE.p: For older releases and change logs, see the changelog page.
The latest source code is available from the git repository at:
